query: Supreme Court Oklahoma $4.5 million federal family planning funds abortion referral mandate ruling

{"query":"Supreme Court Oklahoma $4.5 million federal family planning funds abortion referral mandate ruling","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1267,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.scotusblog.com/2024/09/court-denies-oklahoma-request-to-reinstate-federal-funds-in-dispute-over-abortion-referrals/","title":"Court denies Oklahoma request to reinstate federal funds in dispute over abortion referrals | SCOTUSblog","content":"The Supreme Court on Tuesday denied a request from Oklahoma to reinstate over $4 million in funding for family-planning projects while the state’s challenge to the termination of the grant by the federal Department of Health and Human Services continues in the lower courts.
Court of Appeals for the 10th Circuit upheld that ruling.
The state argued first that requiring family-planning projects to provide counseling and referrals for abortion violates the Constitution’s spending clause, which gives Congress the power to impose and collect taxes.
Congress, she wrote, “routinely conditions federal grants on compliance with requirements contained in agency regulations, and this Court has repeatedly upheld such requirements.”
But Oklahoma countered that Tennessee had not raised the Weldon Amendment as a separate reason to order HHS to reinstate the grant."},{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/supreme-court-wont-restore-oklahoma-family-planning-funds-pulled-after-abortion-law-pushback/","title":"Supreme Court won’t restore Oklahoma family planning funds pulled after abortion law pushback | Courthouse News Service","content":"The justices’ order prevents Oklahoma from claiming a $4.5 million grant the Department of Health and Human Services revoked after the state health department refused to give patients information on abortion.
Justices Clarence Thomas, Samuel Alito and Neil Gorsuch said they would have granted the application.
The Clinton administration reversed the prohibitions to allow abortion counseling.
While Oklahoma’s application was pending, the Sixth Circuit denied a similar appeal from Tennessee."},{"url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/supreme-court/supreme-court-biden-family-planning-oklahoma-abortion-dispute-rcna167345","title":"Supreme Court allows Biden administration to withhold family planning funds to Oklahoma over abortion dispute","content":"The Supreme Court on Tuesday allowed the Biden administration to refuse to disburse federal family planning funds to Oklahoma in an abortion-related dispute.
In 2022, Oklahoma received the funding after complying with the rule, but after the Supreme Court's 2022 ruling that overturned abortion rights landmark Roe v.
Representing the Biden administration, Solicitor General Elizabeth Prelogar wrote in a court filing that the Title X regulation is currently in effect in all 50 states."},{"url":"https://apnews.com/article/supreme-court-oklahoma-family-planning-title-x-3df24e69294f513e68634e6d6a146cf1","title":"Supreme Court won't allow Oklahoma to reclaim federal money in dispute over abortion referrals | AP News","content":"WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Tuesday rejected Oklahoma’s emergency appeal seeking to restore a $4.5 million grant for family planning services in an ongoing dispute over the state’s refusal to refer pregnant women to a nationwide hotline that provides information about abortion and other options.
The case stems from a dispute over state abortion restrictions and federal grants provided under a family planning program known as Title X that has only grown more heated since the Supreme Court overturned Roe v.
The administration said it offered an accommodation that would allow referrals to the national hotline, but the state rejected that as insufficient.
In 2021, the Biden administration reversed a ban on abortion referrals by clinics that accept Title X funds."},{"url":"https://www.kgou.org/health/2026-05-04/supreme-court-gives-abortion-pill-mifepristone-a-1-week-reprieve-from-a-major-change","title":"Supreme Court gives abortion pill mifepristone a 1-week reprieve from a major change | KGOU - Oklahoma's NPR Source","content":"On Friday, the 5th Circuit Court of Appeals, based in New Orleans, issued a 19-page order in the Louisiana case.
Alito blocked the appeals court's decision from going into effect until next Monday, May 11, at 5 p.m."}]}
